Legacy authentication SHALL be blocked.
Users detected as high risk SHALL be blocked.
A notification SHOULD be sent to the administrator when high-risk users are detected.
Sign-ins detected as high risk SHALL be blocked.
Phishing-resistant MFA SHALL be enforced for all users.
If phishing-resistant MFA has not been enforced, an alternative MFA method SHALL be enforced for all users.
If Microsoft Authenticator is enabled, it SHALL be configured to show login context information.
The Authentication Methods Manage Migration feature SHALL be set to Migration Complete.
The authentication methods SMS, Voice Call, and Email One-Time Passcode (OTP) SHALL be disabled.
Phishing-resistant MFA SHALL be required for highly privileged roles.
Managed devices SHOULD be required for authentication.
Managed Devices SHOULD be required to register MFA.
Security logs SHALL be sent to the agency's security operations center for monitoring.
Only administrators SHALL be allowed to register applications.
Only administrators SHALL be allowed to consent to applications.
An admin consent workflow SHALL be configured for applications.
Group owners SHALL NOT be allowed to consent to applications.
User passwords SHALL NOT expire.
A minimum of two users and a maximum of eight users SHALL be provisioned with the Global Administrator role.
Privileged users SHALL be provisioned with finer-grained roles instead of Global Administrator.
Privileged users SHALL be provisioned cloud-only accounts separate from an on-premises directory or other federated identity providers.
Permanent active role assignments SHALL NOT be allowed for highly privileged roles.
Provisioning users to highly privileged roles SHALL NOT occur outside of a PAM system.
Activation of the Global Administrator role SHALL require approval.
Eligible and Active highly privileged role assignments SHALL trigger an alert.
User activation of the Global Administrator role SHALL trigger an alert.
User activation of other highly privileged roles SHOULD trigger an alert.
Guest users SHOULD have limited or restricted access to Azure AD directory objects.
Only users with the Guest Inviter role SHOULD be able to invite guest users.
Guest invites SHOULD only be allowed to specific external domains that have been authorized by the agency for legitimate business purposes.
Automatic forwarding to external domains SHALL be disabled.
A list of approved IP addresses for sending mail SHALL be maintained.
An SPF policy SHALL be published for each domain, designating only these addresses as approved senders.
DKIM SHOULD be enabled for all domains.
A DMARC policy SHALL be published for every second-level domain.
The DMARC message rejection option SHALL be p=reject.
The DMARC point of contact for aggregate reports SHALL include reports@dmarc.cyber.dhs.gov.
SMTP AUTH SHALL be disabled.
Contact folders SHALL NOT be shared with all domains.
Calendar details SHALL NOT be shared with all domains.
External sender warnings SHALL be implemented.
A DLP solution SHALL be used.
The DLP solution SHALL protect personally identifiable information (PII) and sensitive information, as defined by the agency.
The selected DLP solution SHOULD offer services comparable to the native DLP solution offered by Microsoft.
At a minimum, the DLP solution SHALL restrict sharing credit card numbers, U.S. Individual Taxpayer Identification Numbers (ITIN), and U.S. Social Security numbers (SSN) via email.
Emails SHALL be filtered by attachment file types.
The attachment filter SHOULD attempt to determine the true file type and assess the file extension.
Disallowed file types SHALL be determined and enforced.
Alternatively chosen filtering solutions SHOULD offer services comparable to Microsoft Defender's Common Attachment Filter.
At a minimum, click-to-run files SHOULD be blocked (e.g., .exe, .cmd, and .vbe).
Emails SHALL be scanned for malware.
Emails identified as containing malware SHALL be quarantined or dropped.
Email scanning SHALL be capable of reviewing emails after delivery.
Impersonation protection checks SHOULD be used.
User warnings, comparable to the user safety tips included with EOP, SHOULD be displayed.
The phishing protection solution SHOULD include an AI-based phishing detection tool comparable to EOP Mailbox Intelligence.
IP allow lists SHOULD NOT be created.
Safe lists SHOULD NOT be enabled.
Mailbox auditing SHALL be enabled.
A spam filter SHALL be enabled.
Spam and high confidence spam SHALL be moved to either the junk email folder or the quarantine folder.
Allowed domains SHALL NOT be added to inbound anti-spam protection policies.
If a third-party party filtering solution is used, the solution SHOULD offer services comparable to the native spam filtering offered by Microsoft.
URL comparison with a block-list SHOULD be enabled.
Direct download links SHOULD be scanned for malware.
User click tracking SHOULD be enabled.
Alerts SHALL be enabled.
Alerts SHOULD be sent to a monitored address or incorporated into a security information and event management (SIEM) system.
Microsoft Purview Audit (Standard) logging SHALL be enabled.
Microsoft Purview Audit (Premium) logging SHALL be enabled.
Audit logs SHALL be maintained for at least the minimum duration dictated by OMB M-21-31 (Appendix C).
External sharing for SharePoint SHALL be limited to Existing guests or Only People in your organization.
External sharing SHALL be restricted to approved external domains and/or users in approved security groups per interagency collaboration needs.

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Automatic external forwarding must be disabled in Outbound Spam policy
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
IP allow lists in the connection filter bypass all spam and malware filtering for connections from listed IPs. Shared hosting IPs, CDN exit nodes, and business partner ranges can be abused to relay malicious email that completely bypasses filtering.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: Exchange admin center > Protection > Connection filter > Edit the policy and remove all IP allow list entries

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Configure connection filter policies in EOP](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/defender-office-365/connection-filter-policies-configure)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Anonymous users must not be able to start meetings
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Organization-wide shareable links in Power BI grant access to all tenant users, not just intended recipients. Content shared this way is effectively available to the entire tenant.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: Power BI Admin Portal > Tenant settings > Export and sharing > Allow shareable links to grant access to everyone in your organization > Disabled

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Export and sharing settings in the Power BI admin portal](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/admin/service-admin-portal-export-sharing)

Tests.Title: Anonymous users must not be able to use meeting chat
Tests.Name: ISO27001.A.5.14.11: Anonymous users must not be able to use meeting chat
Tests.ScriptBlock:
if (-not $script:meetingPolicy) {
            Set-ItResult -Skipped -Because "Teams meeting policy endpoint not available in this cloud environment"
            return
        }
        $meetingChat = $script:meetingPolicy['meetingChatEnabledType']
        $chatRestricted = $meetingChat -ne 'Enabled'
        $chatRestricted | Should -Be $true `
            -Because "ISO A.5.14 requires meeting chat to exclude anonymous users (set to EnabledExceptAnonymous or Disabled)"

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Anonymous users must not be able to use meeting chat
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Service principal profiles impersonate multiple user identities, complicating audit attribution and allowing access to data belonging to multiple users under a single service principal.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: Power BI Admin Portal > Tenant settings > Developer settings > Allow service principals to create and use profiles > Disabled

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Integration settings in the Power BI admin portal](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/admin/service-admin-portal-integration)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Cloud recording must be disabled by default
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Shareable links that grant access to everyone in the organization allow Power BI content to be accessed by any tenant user who receives the link — regardless of whether they should have access.

#### Remediation action:

Restrict shareable links in Power BI

Configure via: Power BI Admin portal > Tenant settings > Export and sharing settings > Allow shareable links to grant access to everyone in your organization: Disabled or Apply to specific security groups

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Export and sharing settings in the Power BI admin portal](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/admin/service-admin-portal-export-sharing)

Tests.Title: Auto-forwarding to external domains must be disabled
Tests.Name: ISO27001.A.5.14.2: Auto-forwarding to external domains must be disabled
Tests.ScriptBlock:
if (-not $script:exoConnected) {
            Set-ItResult -Skipped -Because "Exchange Online cmdlets not connected"
            return
        }

        $defaultDomain = Get-RemoteDomain -Identity Default -ErrorAction Stop
        $defaultDomain.AutoForwardEnabled | Should -Be $false `
            -Because "ISO A.5.14 requires preventing unauthorized data transfer - auto-forwarding exfiltrates data to external mailboxes"

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Auto-forwarding to external domains must be disabled
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
External mail forwarding creates persistent data exfiltration paths — every email received is silently copied to an external address. Attackers who compromise mailboxes routinely create forwarding rules as a persistence mechanism that survives password resets.

#### Remediation action:

Refer to vendor recommendations for correcting this setting.

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Control automatic external email forwarding in Microsoft 365](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/defender-office-365/outbound-spam-policies-external-email-forwarding)

Tests.Title: SharePoint external sharing must not be set to 'Anyone with link'
Tests.Name: ISO27001.A.5.14.3: SharePoint external sharing must not be set to 'Anyone with link'
Tests.ScriptBlock:
if (-not $script:spoSettings) {
            Set-ItResult -Skipped -Because "SharePoint settings not available - requires SharePointTenantSettings.Read.All"
            return
        }
        $sharingCapability = $script:spoSettings['sharingCapability']
        $sharingCapability | Should -Not -Be 'externalUserAndGuestSharing' `
            -Because "ISO A.5.14 requires external sharing to be restricted - 'Anyone with link' allows unauthenticated access to data"

ResultDetail.TestTitle: SharePoint external sharing must not be set to 'Anyone with link'
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Site-level sharing settings cannot exceed the tenant-wide sharing policy. If the tenant policy allows sharing with anyone, individual sites must restrict sharing themselves. Without a restrictive tenant policy, every new site defaults to maximum sharing permissions.

#### Remediation action:

Ensure SharePointTenantSettings.Read.All permission is consented. Review site sharing levels in

Configure via: SharePoint admin center > Active sites

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Manage sharing settings for SharePoint and OneDrive](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sharepoint/turn-external-sharing-on-or-off)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: External users must not be able to re-share content
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Channel email addresses allow external parties to post messages to Teams channels by sending email to a generated address. This bypasses normal message review and creates an inbound channel that is difficult to monitor for malicious attachments or phishing content.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: Teams admin center > Teams settings > Email integration > Users can send emails to a channel email address > Off

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Teams update process](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoftteams/teams-client-update)

Tests.Title: Sharing domain restriction must be configured (allow or block list)
Tests.Name: ISO27001.A.5.14.5: Sharing domain restriction must be configured (allow or block list)
Tests.ScriptBlock:
if (-not $script:spoSettings) {
            Set-ItResult -Skipped -Because "SharePoint settings not available"
            return
        }
        $domainRestriction = $script:spoSettings['sharingDomainRestrictionMode']
        $isRestricted = $domainRestriction -in @('allowList', 'blockList')
        $isRestricted | Should -Be $true `
            -Because "ISO A.5.14 requires limiting external sharing to trusted domains only via allow or block list"

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Sharing domain restriction must be configured (allow or block list)
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
IP allow lists in the connection filter bypass all spam and malware filtering for connections from listed IPs. Shared hosting IPs, CDN exit nodes, and business partner ranges can be abused to relay malicious email that completely bypasses filtering.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: Exchange admin center > Protection > Connection filter > Edit the policy and remove all IP allow list entries

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Configure connection filter policies in EOP](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/defender-office-365/connection-filter-policies-configure)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Guest access expiration must be enabled and set to 30 days or less
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Auto-labeling policies classify sensitive content without requiring user action, ensuring that documents containing PII, financial data, or health information are consistently classified and subject to appropriate access and retention controls.

#### Remediation action:

Requires Azure Information Protection P2 (E5 or E5 Compliance)

Configure via: Microsoft Purview > Information protection > Auto-labeling > Create a policy to automatically classify sensitive content

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Apply a sensitivity label automatically](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/apply-sensitivity-label-automatically)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: SharePoint/OneDrive B2B integration must be enabled for Entra-managed guest access
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Guest access links that never expire create permanent access for external users, regardless of whether the collaboration purpose is still active. An expiry ensures access is automatically revoked after a defined period.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: SharePoint admin center > Policies > Sharing > Guest access expiration

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Manage sharing settings for SharePoint and OneDrive](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sharepoint/sharepoint-online/turn-external-sharing-on-or-off)

Tests.Title: External Teams domain access must be disabled or restricted to specific domains
Tests.Name: ISO27001.A.5.14.8: External Teams domain access must be disabled or restricted to specific domains
Tests.ScriptBlock:
if (-not $script:teamsClientConfig) {
            Set-ItResult -Skipped -Because "Teams client config endpoint not available in this cloud environment"
            return
        }
        $allowFederated = $script:teamsClientConfig['allowFederatedUsers']
        $allowedDomains = $script:teamsClientConfig['allowedDomains']
        $isRestricted = (-not $allowFederated) -or ($allowedDomains -and $allowedDomains.Count -gt 0)
        $isRestricted | Should -Be $true `
            -Because "ISO A.5.14 requires external collaboration to be limited to trusted domains only"

ResultDetail.TestTitle: External Teams domain access must be disabled or restricted to specific domains
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
DKIM signs outbound messages with a domain-specific cryptographic key, allowing receiving servers to verify that mail was not tampered with in transit and genuinely originated from the domain. Without DKIM, spoofed messages claiming to be from the domain will pass unauthenticated and may deceive recipients.

#### Remediation action:

Connect to Exchange Online and verify accepted domains

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Set up DKIM to sign mail from your Microsoft 365 domain](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/security/office-365-security/email-authentication-dkim-configure)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Anonymous users must not be able to join meetings
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Organization-wide shareable links in Power BI grant access to all tenant users, not just intended recipients. Content shared this way is effectively available to the entire tenant.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: Power BI Admin Portal > Tenant settings > Export and sharing > Allow shareable links to grant access to everyone in your organization > Disabled

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Export and sharing settings in the Power BI admin portal](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/admin/service-admin-portal-export-sharing)

Tests.Block: ISO 27001 - Entra ID Password & MFA Settings

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Security Defaults or equivalent Conditional Access policies must be enabled
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Security Defaults enforce a baseline of MFA for all users and block legacy authentication — but they are incompatible with Conditional Access and are typically disabled when CA policies are in place. A tenant with Security Defaults disabled and insufficient CA policies has no enforced authentication baseline.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: Entra admin center > Properties > Manage security defaults

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Security defaults in Microsoft Entra ID](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/fundamentals/security-defaults)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: ResourceKey authentication must be blocked
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Standard MFA (TOTP, push notification, SMS) is bypassed by AiTM phishing toolkits (e.g., Evilginx, Modlishka) that relay authentication in real time and capture session cookies. Phishing-resistant MFA (FIDO2, Windows Hello, certificate-based) is cryptographically bound to the origin and cannot be relayed by an attacker-controlled proxy.

#### Remediation action:

Create a CA policy: Target admin roles > Grant > Require authentication strength > Phishing-resistant MFA

Configure via: Entra admin center > Protection > Conditional Access

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Authentication strengths overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/authentication/concept-authentication-strengths)

- [Microsoft Learn — Enable FIDO2 security key sign-in](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/authentication/howto-authentication-passwordless-security-key)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Service Principal API access must be disabled or restricted
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Unrestricted service principal access to Power BI APIs enables any registered app to query and manipulate Power BI content programmatically. Compromised service principal credentials provide silent, authenticated API access.

#### Remediation action:

Restrict API access to approved service principals only. . Create a group containing only approved service principals

Configure via: Power BI Admin portal > Tenant settings > Developer settings > Allow service principals to use Power BI APIs: Apply to specific security groups

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Developer settings in the Power BI admin portal](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/admin/service-admin-portal-developer)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Users must not be allowed to create security groups
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Applications that impersonate Microsoft product names in their display names are a social engineering vector. Users who see 'Microsoft Teams Meeting Add-in' or 'Office 365 Security' in a consent prompt are more likely to approve the consent without scrutiny.

#### Remediation action:

Investigate foreign apps using Microsoft product names -- they may be social engineering attempts. Verify the publisher and appId against known Microsoft first-party apps. Remove if suspicious

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Unexpected error when performing consent to an application](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/enterprise-apps/application-sign-in-unexpected-user-consent-error)

Tests.Title: OWA additional storage providers must be disabled
Tests.Name: ISO27001.A.5.16.2: OWA additional storage providers must be disabled
Tests.ScriptBlock:
if (-not $script:exoConnected) {
            Set-ItResult -Skipped -Because "Exchange Online cmdlets not connected"
            return
        }

        $owaPolicies = Get-OwaMailboxPolicy -ErrorAction Stop -WarningAction SilentlyContinue
        $enabledStorage = @($owaPolicies | Where-Object { $_.AdditionalStorageProvidersAvailable -eq $true })
        $enabledStorage.Count | Should -Be 0 `
            -Because "ISO A.5.16 requires restricting data storage to approved locations - OWA third-party storage providers bypass corporate data governance"

ResultDetail.TestTitle: OWA additional storage providers must be disabled
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Permanent eligible assignments allow role activation at any time without expiry. Time-bound eligible assignments limit the window during which a compromised account can activate a privileged role — after expiry, the assignment must be renewed through a governed process.

#### Remediation action:

Set a time-bound expiry on eligible assignments for Tier-0 roles. . Remove existing permanent eligible assignments and reissue as time-bound

Configure via: Entra admin center > Identity Governance > PIM > Microsoft Entra roles > select role > Settings > Edit > Assignment tab > Expire eligible assignments after (e.g., 12 months)

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Configure Microsoft Entra role settings in PIM](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/id-governance/privileged-identity-management/pim-how-to-change-default-settings)

- [Microsoft Learn — Assign Microsoft Entra roles in PIM](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/id-governance/privileged-identity-management/pim-how-to-add-role-to-user)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Default role assignment policy must not allow user-installed Outlook add-ins
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Permanent eligible assignments allow role activation at any time without expiry. Time-bound eligible assignments limit the window during which a compromised account can activate a privileged role — after expiry, the assignment must be renewed through a governed process.

#### Remediation action:

Set a time-bound expiry on eligible assignments for Tier-0 roles. . Remove existing permanent eligible assignments and reissue as time-bound

Configure via: Entra admin center > Identity Governance > PIM > Microsoft Entra roles > select role > Settings > Edit > Assignment tab > Expire eligible assignments after (e.g., 12 months)

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Configure Microsoft Entra role settings in PIM](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/id-governance/privileged-identity-management/pim-how-to-change-default-settings)

- [Microsoft Learn — Assign Microsoft Entra roles in PIM](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/id-governance/privileged-identity-management/pim-how-to-add-role-to-user)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Invite external users permission must be disabled for guests
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Security Defaults enforce a baseline of MFA for all users and block legacy authentication — but they are incompatible with Conditional Access and are typically disabled when CA policies are in place. A tenant with Security Defaults disabled and insufficient CA policies has no enforced authentication baseline.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: Entra admin center > Properties > Manage security defaults

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Security defaults in Microsoft Entra ID](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/fundamentals/security-defaults)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Guest access to organizational content must be disabled/restricted
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
SharePoint is a primary repository for sensitive organizational documents. Unrestricted external sharing allows any user to share any document with any email address, bypassing data classification controls and creating permanent unauthenticated access links.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: SharePoint admin center > Policies > Sharing

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Manage sharing settings for SharePoint and OneDrive](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sharepoint/turn-external-sharing-on-or-off)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Operational Global Administrators count must be between 2 and 4
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Having too few Global Administrators risks loss of access if credentials are unavailable. Having too many increases the attack surface — each GA account is a potential full-tenant compromise. Two to four is the recommended balance for coverage without excessive exposure.

#### Remediation action:

The Global Administrator directory role is not activated in this tenant. Activate the role by assigning at least one user, then re-run the assessment

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Best practices for Microsoft Entra roles](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/role-based-access-control/best-practices)

- [Microsoft Learn — About admin roles in the Microsoft 365 admin center](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/admin/add-users/about-admin-roles)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: PIM must manage Global Administrator role (no permanent standing assignments)
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Permanently active privileged roles maintain standing admin access with no time limit or justification requirement. JIT access via PIM limits the window of exposure: even if admin credentials are compromised, the attacker can only act during the brief activation window.

#### Remediation action:

Use eligible assignments with time-bound activation

Configure via: Entra admin center > Identity Governance > Privileged Identity Management > Azure AD roles > Global Administrator > Remove permanent active assignments

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — What is Microsoft Entra Privileged Identity Management?](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/id-governance/privileged-identity-management/pim-configure)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Guest access reviews must be configured
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Access reviews for guest users detect guests who no longer need tenant access. Without reviews, guest accounts accumulate over time and may retain access long after the collaboration project or relationship that required it has ended.

#### Remediation action:

Schedule recurring reviews

Configure via: Entra admin center > Identity Governance > Access reviews > New access review > Review type: Guest users only

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Assign Microsoft Entra roles in Privileged Identity Management](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/id-governance/privileged-identity-management/pim-how-to-add-role-to-user)

- [Microsoft Learn — Configure security alerts for Microsoft Entra roles in PIM](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/id-governance/privileged-identity-management/pim-configure-security-alerts)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Privileged roles access reviews must be configured
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Access reviews for privileged roles detect and remove stale, unnecessary, or unauthorized role assignments. Without periodic reviews, accumulation of privileged role assignments creates lateral movement opportunities and violates least-privilege principles.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: Entra admin center > Identity Governance > Access reviews > New access review > Review type: Members of a group or Users assigned to a privileged role

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Create an access review of Azure resource and Microsoft Entra roles in PIM](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/id-governance/privileged-identity-management/pim-create-roles-and-resource-roles-review)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Global Administrator activation must require approval
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
The Global Administrator role provides complete control over the tenant. Requiring approval for activation means that even a compromised account cannot self-activate the role — an approver must confirm the activation request before access is granted.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: Entra admin center > Identity Governance > PIM > Azure AD roles > Settings > Global Administrator > Require approval to activate > Yes

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Configure Microsoft Entra role settings in PIM](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/id-governance/privileged-identity-management/pim-how-to-change-default-settings)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: All Global Administrator accounts must be cloud-only
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Global Administrators control every aspect of the tenant. Standard MFA can be bypassed via adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM) phishing attacks that relay the authentication session. Phishing-resistant MFA (FIDO2, Windows Hello, certificate) eliminates this attack class for the highest-privilege accounts.

#### Remediation action:

Enroll all Global Administrators in phishing-resistant MFA (FIDO2, Windows Hello for Business, or certificate-based)

Configure via: Entra admin center > Protection > Authentication methods > Policies

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Authentication strengths overview](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/authentication/concept-authentication-strengths)

- [Microsoft Learn — Enable FIDO2 security key sign-in](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/authentication/howto-authentication-passwordless-security-key)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Global Administrators must not be assigned full M365 E3/E5 productivity licenses
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Admin accounts with full E3/E5 licenses have access to productivity services (Exchange, Teams, SharePoint) which increase their attack surface — more services to phish, more sessions to steal. A minimal license reduces the services accessible via a compromised admin credential.

#### Remediation action:

Assign admin accounts minimal licenses (Entra ID P2). Do not assign E3/E5 productivity suites

Configure via: M365 admin center > Users > Active users > Licenses

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Best practices for Microsoft Entra roles](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/role-based-access-control/best-practices)

- [Microsoft Learn — Assign admin roles in the Microsoft 365 admin center](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/admin/misc/microsoft-365-admin-role-guidance)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Communication with unmanaged Teams users (personal accounts) must be disabled
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Communication with unmanaged Teams users (accounts not backed by an organization) allows contact from individuals with no organizational accountability or identity verification.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: Teams admin center > Users > External access > Teams accounts not managed by an organization > Off

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Manage external access in Microsoft Teams](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoftteams/manage-external-access)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: External unmanaged Teams users must not be able to initiate conversations
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Allowing external Teams users from any organization to initiate conversations with internal users creates an unsolicited contact channel from any organization worldwide, including potential threat actors conducting reconnaissance or social engineering.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: Teams admin center > Users > External access > External users can initiate conversations > Off

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Manage external access in Microsoft Teams](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoftteams/manage-external-access)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: At least one data retention policy must be configured and active
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Teams messages and meeting recordings constitute business records subject to the same retention requirements as email. Without a retention policy, Teams data can be permanently deleted, destroying evidence of conversations, decisions, and security-relevant activity.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: Microsoft Purview > Data lifecycle management > Retention policies > Create or edit a retention policy to include Teams channel messages and chats

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Learn about retention for Microsoft Teams](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/retention-policies-teams)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Exchange locations must be covered by a retention policy
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Email is the primary record of business communications and the most common target in litigation holds and regulatory audits. Without a retention policy, email data may be deleted before the legally required retention period, creating compliance exposure and destroying forensic evidence.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: Microsoft Purview > Data lifecycle management > Retention policies > Create or edit a retention policy to include Exchange email and mailboxes

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Learn about retention for Exchange](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/retention-policies-exchange)

- [Microsoft Learn — Create and configure retention policies](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/create-retention-policies)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: SharePoint and OneDrive locations must be covered by a retention policy
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
SharePoint and OneDrive store documents, collaboration artifacts, and file-based records. Without a retention policy, files can be deleted before their required retention period expires, eliminating records needed for compliance, litigation, and forensic investigations.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: Microsoft Purview > Data lifecycle management > Retention policies > Create or edit a retention policy to include SharePoint sites and OneDrive accounts

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Learn about retention for SharePoint and OneDrive](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/retention-policies-sharepoint)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Enabled retention policies must be in Enforce mode (not simulation/test)
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Data retention policies ensure that content is preserved for the required period and disposed of afterward. Without retention policies, data subject to legal holds or regulatory requirements can be prematurely deleted or retained indefinitely without governance.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: Microsoft Purview > Data lifecycle management > Retention policies > Create a retention policy to preserve or delete content per your requirements

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Learn about retention for Microsoft Teams](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/retention-policies-teams)

- [Microsoft Learn — Create and configure retention policies](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/create-retention-policies)

Tests.Title: Publish to Web (anonymous access) must be disabled
Tests.Name: ISO27001.A.8.12.1: Publish to Web (anonymous access) must be disabled
Tests.ScriptBlock:
if (-not $script:pbiCmdAvailable -or $script:allSettings.Count -eq 0) {
            Set-ItResult -Skipped -Because "Power BI admin API not connected or not available in this session"
            return
        }

        # In Power BI API, this is True if disabled (i.e. WebDashboardsPublishToWebDisabled)
        $publishToWebDisabled = Get-TestTenantSetting -SettingName 'WebDashboardsPublishToWebDisabled'
        $publishToWebDisabled | Should -Be $true -Because "ISO A.8.12 / A.8.13 require blocking public publish to web to prevent anonymous exposure of internal reports"

- [Microsoft Learn — Export and sharing settings in the Power BI admin portal](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/admin/service-admin-portal-export-sharing)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Shareable links to entire organization must be disabled
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Organization-wide shareable links in Power BI grant access to all tenant users, not just intended recipients. Content shared this way is effectively available to the entire tenant.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: Power BI Admin Portal > Tenant settings > Export and sharing > Allow shareable links to grant access to everyone in your organization > Disabled

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Export and sharing settings in the Power BI admin portal](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/admin/service-admin-portal-export-sharing)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: External data sharing receiver collaboration must be disabled
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Sites containing sensitive data require tighter sharing controls than general-purpose collaboration sites. Without site-level sharing restrictions, sensitive data sites are subject to the same sharing rules as non-sensitive sites, potentially allowing external sharing of regulated content.

#### Remediation action:

Review site sharing manually in

Configure via: SharePoint admin center > Active sites

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Manage sharing settings for SharePoint and OneDrive](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sharepoint/turn-external-sharing-on-or-off)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: R and Python custom visuals must be disabled
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Personally owned device enrollment without restrictions allows unmanaged personal devices to be enrolled in MDM, where they receive corporate app configurations and access to corporate data. Without restrictions, any device — including insecure personal devices — can become a corporate endpoint.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: Intune admin center > Devices > Enrollment > Device platform restrictions > Edit default policy > Block personally owned devices for each platform

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — What is device enrollment in Microsoft Intune?](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/enrollment/device-enrollment)

- [Microsoft Learn — Create enrollment restrictions in Microsoft Intune](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/intune/enrollment/enrollment-restrictions-set)

        }

            -Because "ISO A.8.15 requires audit logging to be enabled for security monitoring and incident investigation"

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Exchange organization-level auditing must not be disabled
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Mailbox audit logging is foundational for incident response in Exchange Online. Disabling audit at the organization level creates gaps in the forensic record that cannot be reconstructed after an incident, leaving investigations without evidence of data access or exfiltration.

#### Remediation action:

Refer to vendor recommendations for correcting this setting.

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Manage mailbox auditing](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/compliance/audit-mailboxes)

- [Microsoft Learn — Turn auditing on or off](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/audit-log-enable-disable)

Tests.Title: No mailboxes should have audit bypass enabled
Tests.Name: ISO27001.A.8.15.2: No mailboxes should have audit bypass enabled
Tests.ScriptBlock:
if (-not $script:exoConnected) {
            Set-ItResult -Skipped -Because "Exchange Online cmdlets not connected"
            return
        }

        $bypassed = @(Get-MailboxAuditBypassAssociation -ResultSize Unlimited -ErrorAction Stop -WarningAction SilentlyContinue |
            Where-Object { $_.AuditBypassEnabled -eq $true })
        $bypassed.Count | Should -Be 0 `
            -Because "ISO A.8.15 requires complete audit trails - mailbox audit bypass creates unmonitored accounts"

ResultDetail.TestTitle: No mailboxes should have audit bypass enabled
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Audit bypass allows specific mailboxes to take actions without generating audit log entries. This capability was designed for service accounts during high-volume mail processing but can be abused to hide malicious mail access, data exfiltration, or lateral movement activity.

#### Remediation action:

Refer to vendor recommendations for correcting this setting.

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Manage mailbox auditing](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/compliance/audit-mailboxes)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Mailbox auditing must be enabled on all user mailboxes (sample)
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Mailboxes hidden from the Global Address List (GAL) cannot be found through normal directory lookup, which may indicate misconfiguration, legacy service accounts with active mailboxes, or deliberate concealment. All active mailboxes should be visible and accounted for in the directory.

#### Remediation action:

Investigate hidden user mailboxes. Mailboxes hidden from the Global Address List may indicate a compromised account. Review: Get-Mailbox -Filter "HiddenFromAddressListsEnabled -eq $true -and RecipientTypeDetails -eq UserMailbox"

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Hide recipients from address lists in Exchange Online](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/exchange/recipients-in-exchange-online/manage-user-mailboxes/hide-from-address-lists)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Unified Audit Log Ingestion must be enabled
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Audit log search is the investigative capability for Microsoft 365 — every admin action, user sign-in, file access, and configuration change is recorded here. Without it enabled, incidents cannot be investigated.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: Microsoft Purview > Audit > Start recording user and admin activity

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Turn auditing on or off](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/audit-log-enable-disable)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Windows Autopilot or device enrollment program must be configured
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Retention policies in simulation mode do not enforce retention — they only predict what would be retained. Policies must be in Enforce mode to actually preserve or delete content according to the configured rules.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: Microsoft Purview > Data lifecycle management > Retention policies > Edit each policy in simulation mode and switch it to Enforce once validated

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Create and configure retention policies](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/create-retention-policies)

- [Microsoft Learn — Learn about retention policies and retention labels](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/retention)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Microsoft Entra Join permissions must be restricted (not set to All)
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Applications with Tier 0 permissions (e.g., Directory.ReadWrite.All, RoleManagement.ReadWrite.Directory) can read and modify the entire Entra ID directory, including assigning admin roles to other principals. A compromised app credential is equivalent to a compromised Global Administrator.

#### Remediation action:

Each has a documented path to Global Administrator. Replace with narrower permissions or use managed identities where possible

Configure via: Entra admin center > App registrations > review internal apps with Tier 0 permissions

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Grant tenant-wide admin consent](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/enterprise-apps/grant-admin-consent)

- [Microsoft Learn — Privileged access: enterprise access model (Tier 0)](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/privileged-access-workstations/privileged-access-access-model)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: All shared mailbox accounts must have sign-in blocked
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Applications with dangerous application permissions (not delegated) act as autonomous service accounts that can read, write, and delete data for all users without any user sign-in. Foreign (multi-tenant) apps with these permissions operate from outside the tenant's control plane.

#### Remediation action:

These permissions have documented attack paths to Global Administrator. Remove or replace with least-privilege alternatives

Configure via: Entra admin center > Enterprise applications > review foreign apps with Tier 0 permissions

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Manage and review permissions granted to enterprise apps](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/enterprise-apps/manage-application-permissions)

- [Microsoft Learn — Microsoft Graph permissions reference](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/permissions-reference)

Tests.Title: Modern Authentication must be enabled
Tests.Name: ISO27001.A.8.20.1: Modern Authentication must be enabled
Tests.ScriptBlock:
if (-not $script:exoConnected) {
            Set-ItResult -Skipped -Because "Exchange Online cmdlets not connected"
            return
        }

        $orgConfig = Get-OrganizationConfig -ErrorAction Stop
        $orgConfig.OAuth2ClientProfileEnabled | Should -Be $true `
            -Because "ISO A.8.20 requires modern auth protocols to prevent credential theft via legacy auth"

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Modern Authentication must be enabled
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
IP-based named locations used in CA 'trusted location' exclusions can be spoofed via VPN or proxy. Relying on IP reputation as a trust signal without combining it with device compliance or MFA creates an authentication bypass for any attacker who can route through a trusted IP range.

#### Remediation action:

IP-based trusted locations can be spoofed via VPN or proxy. Consider Global Secure Access compliant network checks or country-based locations for stronger assurance

Configure via: Entra admin center > Protection > Conditional Access > Named locations

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Location condition in Conditional Access](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/conditional-access/location-condition)

Tests.Title: SMTP AUTH must be disabled org-wide
Tests.Name: ISO27001.A.8.20.2: SMTP AUTH must be disabled org-wide
Tests.ScriptBlock:
if (-not $script:exoConnected) {
            Set-ItResult -Skipped -Because "Exchange Online cmdlets not connected"
            return
        }

        $transportConfig = Get-TransportConfig -ErrorAction Stop
        $transportConfig.SmtpClientAuthenticationDisabled | Should -Be $true `
            -Because "ISO A.8.20 requires disabling legacy SMTP AUTH which bypasses MFA and modern auth controls"

ResultDetail.TestTitle: SMTP AUTH must be disabled org-wide
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Site-level sharing settings cannot exceed the tenant-wide sharing policy. If the tenant policy allows sharing with anyone, individual sites must restrict sharing themselves. Without a restrictive tenant policy, every new site defaults to maximum sharing permissions.

#### Remediation action:

Ensure SharePointTenantSettings.Read.All permission is consented. Review site sharing levels in

Configure via: SharePoint admin center > Active sites

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Manage sharing settings for SharePoint and OneDrive](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sharepoint/turn-external-sharing-on-or-off)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Legacy authentication protocols must be disabled in SharePoint
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
IP-based named locations used in CA 'trusted location' exclusions can be spoofed via VPN or proxy. Relying on IP reputation as a trust signal without combining it with device compliance or MFA creates an authentication bypass for any attacker who can route through a trusted IP range.

#### Remediation action:

IP-based trusted locations can be spoofed via VPN or proxy. Consider Global Secure Access compliant network checks or country-based locations for stronger assurance

Configure via: Entra admin center > Protection > Conditional Access > Named locations

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Location condition in Conditional Access](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/conditional-access/location-condition)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Third-party cloud storage providers must be disabled in Teams
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Allowing third-party storage services in Microsoft 365 apps enables users to save and open files from unmanaged cloud services within the same session as corporate data. Files saved to personal cloud storage bypass organizational DLP and retention policies.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: M365 admin center > Settings > Org settings > Microsoft 365 on the web > uncheck all third-party storage services

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Configure how users consent to applications](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/enterprise-apps/configure-user-consent)

ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Allowing additional storage providers in Outlook on the Web enables users to attach and save files from personal cloud storage services within the corporate web mail session. This creates a pathway for sensitive email content to be saved to personal storage outside organizational controls.

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Phishing threshold, Spoof Intel, and DMARC policies must be configured
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Auto-labeling policies classify sensitive content without requiring user action, ensuring that documents containing PII, financial data, or health information are consistently classified and subject to appropriate access and retention controls.

#### Remediation action:

Requires Azure Information Protection P2 (E5 or E5 Compliance)

Configure via: Microsoft Purview > Information protection > Auto-labeling > Create a policy to automatically classify sensitive content

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Apply a sensitivity label automatically](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/apply-sensitivity-label-automatically)

Tests.Title: No transport rules should whitelist domains by bypassing spam filtering (SCL=-1)
Tests.Name: ISO27001.A.8.21.10: No transport rules should whitelist domains by bypassing spam filtering (SCL=-1)
Tests.ScriptBlock:
if (-not $script:exoConnected) {
            Set-ItResult -Skipped -Because "Exchange Online cmdlets not connected"
            return
        }

        $transportRules = Get-TransportRule -ErrorAction Stop
        $whitelistRules = @($transportRules | Where-Object { $_.SetSCL -eq -1 -and $_.SenderDomainIs })
        $whitelistRules.Count | Should -Be 0 `
            -Because "ISO A.8.21 requires all email to pass anti-spam - SCL=-1 rules create bypass paths for malicious email"

ResultDetail.TestTitle: No transport rules should whitelist domains by bypassing spam filtering (SCL=-1)
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Conditional Access policies that target directory roles by name only protect role members at policy evaluation time. Active role assignments added after policy creation are not covered until the policy explicitly includes them. Tier-0 roles without CA coverage are high-privilege accounts with no authentication requirements enforced by CA.

#### Remediation action:

Consider creating CA policies that specifically target privileged directory roles with stricter controls (phishing-resistant MFA, compliant devices)

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Users and groups in Conditional Access policy](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/conditional-access/concept-conditional-access-users-groups)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: No inbound connectors should allow unauthenticated relay (no TLS + no domain restriction)
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Direct send allows unauthenticated email delivery from internal IP ranges without credentials. Without controls on which systems are authorized to send, any device that can reach Exchange Online can send email appearing to originate from your domain — a spoofing and BEC enabler.

#### Remediation action:

Connect to Exchange Online PowerShell to check inbound connector configuration

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — How to set up a multifunction device to send email using Microsoft 365](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/exchange/mail-flow-best-practices/how-to-set-up-a-multifunction-device-or-application-to-send-email-using-microsoft-365-or-office-365)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Bulk threshold, spam actions, and ZAP must be enabled
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
IP allow lists in the connection filter bypass all spam and malware filtering for connections from listed IPs. Shared hosting IPs, CDN exit nodes, and business partner ranges can be abused to relay malicious email that completely bypasses filtering.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: Exchange admin center > Protection > Connection filter > Edit the policy and remove all IP allow list entries

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Configure connection filter policies in EOP](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/defender-office-365/connection-filter-policies-configure)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Safe Links URL scanning and click tracking must be enabled
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Microsoft Secure Score measures the organization's security posture against a set of recommended controls. Without actively tracking and improving it, remediation of security gaps is not systematically prioritized.

#### Remediation action:

Review and act on improvement actions. Microsoft Secure Score > . Assign owners to high-impact actions and track progress

Configure via: security.microsoft.com/securescore > Improvement actions > sort by Score impact

#### Related links

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Safe Attachments policy must be enabled and set to block malicious files
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Allowing additional storage providers in Outlook on the Web enables users to attach and save files from personal cloud storage services within the corporate web mail session. This creates a pathway for sensitive email content to be saved to personal storage outside organizational controls.

#### Remediation action:

Refer to vendor recommendations for correcting this setting.

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Outlook on the web mailbox policy procedures in Exchange Online](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/exchange/clients-and-mobile-in-exchange-online/outlook-on-the-web/outlook-on-the-web-mailbox-policy-procedures)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Safe Attachments for SharePoint, OneDrive, and Teams must be enabled
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Channel email addresses allow external parties to post messages to Teams channels by sending email to a generated address. This bypasses normal message review and creates an inbound channel that is difficult to monitor for malicious attachments or phishing content.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: Teams admin center > Teams settings > Email integration > Users can send emails to a channel email address > Off

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Teams update process](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoftteams/teams-client-update)

if (-not $script:exoConnected) {
            Set-ItResult -Skipped -Because "Exchange Online cmdlets not connected"
            return
        }

        $externalInOutlook = Get-ExternalInOutlook -ErrorAction Stop
        $externalInOutlook.Enabled | Should -Be $true `
            -Because "ISO A.8.21 requires users to be able to identify untrusted external senders to prevent phishing"

ResultDetail.TestTitle: External sender tagging must be enabled to identify phishing attempts
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Allowing file sharing from any cloud storage service enables users to share files from personal cloud services in Teams, moving data outside the organizational boundary without DLP inspection.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: Teams admin center > Messaging policies > Manage third-party storage

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Teams update process](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoftteams/teams-client-update)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Connection filter IP Allow List must be empty (no IPs bypass spam filtering)
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
IP allow lists in the connection filter bypass all spam and malware filtering for connections from listed IPs. Shared hosting IPs, CDN exit nodes, and business partner ranges can be abused to relay malicious email that completely bypasses filtering.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: Exchange admin center > Protection > Connection filter > Edit the policy and remove all IP allow list entries

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Configure connection filter policies in EOP](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/defender-office-365/connection-filter-policies-configure)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Connection filter safe list must be disabled
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
IP allow lists in the connection filter bypass all spam and malware filtering for connections from listed IPs. Shared hosting IPs, CDN exit nodes, and business partner ranges can be abused to relay malicious email that completely bypasses filtering.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: Exchange admin center > Protection > Connection filter > Edit the policy and remove all IP allow list entries

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Configure connection filter policies in EOP](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/defender-office-365/connection-filter-policies-configure)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Sensitivity labels must be enabled in Power BI
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Sensitivity labels on Power BI content propagate classification metadata to exported files. Without label support, data exported from Power BI loses its sensitivity classification and associated DLP and encryption protections.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: Power BI Admin Portal > Tenant settings > Information protection > Allow users to apply sensitivity labels for content > Enabled

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Sensitivity labels in Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-security-sensitivity-label-overview)

- [Microsoft Learn — Data protection in Power BI](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/enterprise/service-security-data-protection-overview)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Restrict non-admin users from accessing Microsoft Entra admin center
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Allowing non-admin users to create new Entra tenants enables them to establish separate identity environments outside organizational governance. Rogue tenants can be used to exfiltrate data or establish shadow IT identity providers.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: Entra admin center > Users > User settings

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — What is Microsoft Entra ID?](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/fundamentals/whatis)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Global Administrator role must not be added as local administrator on Entra-joined devices
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
The Global Administrator role added as a local admin on all Entra-joined devices means that a compromised GA account has local administrator rights across every managed Windows device — far exceeding the access needed for cloud administration.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: Entra admin center > Devices > Device settings > Global administrator is added as local administrator on the device during Azure AD Join > No

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — How to plan your Microsoft Entra join implementation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/devices/device-join-plan)

Tests.Title: Default sharing link type must be 'Specific people' (not Anyone or Organization)
Tests.Name: ISO27001.A.8.3.3: Default sharing link type must be 'Specific people' (not Anyone or Organization)
Tests.ScriptBlock:
if (-not $script:spoSettings) {
            Set-ItResult -Skipped -Because "SharePoint settings not available"
            return
        }
        $defaultLinkType = $script:spoSettings['defaultSharingLinkType']
        $defaultLinkType | Should -Be 'specificPeople' `
            -Because "ISO A.8.3 requires the default sharing link to require explicit recipient selection - not broad organization or anonymous links"

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Default sharing link type must be 'Specific people' (not Anyone or Organization)
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Shareable links that grant access to 'anyone with the link' bypass all identity and device controls. A single leaked link is sufficient to expose the target content to any person, device, or automated system worldwide.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: SharePoint admin center > Policies > Sharing > File and folder links > Default link type > Specific people

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Manage sharing settings for SharePoint and OneDrive](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sharepoint/turn-external-sharing-on-or-off)

- [Microsoft Learn — Change the default link type for SharePoint and OneDrive](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sharepoint/change-default-sharing-link)

            Set-ItResult -Skipped -Because "SharePoint settings not available"
            return
        }
        $defaultPerm = $script:spoSettings['defaultLinkPermission']
        $defaultPerm | Should -Be 'view' `
            -Because "ISO A.8.3 requires the principle of least privilege - default sharing should grant read-only access"

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Default sharing link permission must be 'View' (not Edit)
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
The default sharing link type determines what permission is granted when a user creates a new sharing link without explicitly choosing permissions. A View-only default prevents inadvertent Edit access grants.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: SharePoint admin center > Policies > Sharing > File and folder links > Default permission > View

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Manage sharing settings for SharePoint and OneDrive](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sharepoint/sharepoint-online/turn-external-sharing-on-or-off)

        }

        $unmanagedSync | Should -Be $true `

ResultDetail.TestTitle: OneDrive/SharePoint sync from unmanaged devices must be blocked
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Allowing OneDrive sync from unmanaged devices copies all synced files to devices without organizational security controls. Personal devices sync corporate files locally without BitLocker, AV, or endpoint security enforcement.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: SharePoint admin center > Settings > Sync > Allow syncing only on computers joined to specific domains

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Allow syncing only on computers joined to specific domains](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sharepoint/allow-syncing-only-on-specific-domains)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Idle session timeout policy must be configured
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Idle session timeout for SharePoint disconnects sessions that have been inactive, reducing the risk of unauthorized access on shared or unattended workstations. Without timeout, an authenticated SharePoint session on an unlocked machine remains active indefinitely.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: M365 admin center > Settings > Org settings > Idle session timeout

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Sign out inactive users](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sharepoint/sharepoint-online/sign-out-inactive-users)

Tests.Title: Lobby bypass must be restricted to org members only (not everyone)
Tests.Name: ISO27001.A.8.4.3: Lobby bypass must be restricted to org members only (not everyone)
Tests.ScriptBlock:
if (-not $script:meetingPolicy) {
            Set-ItResult -Skipped -Because "Teams meeting policy endpoint not available in this cloud environment"
            return
        }
        $autoAdmit = $script:meetingPolicy['autoAdmittedUsers']
        $isRestricted = $autoAdmit -eq 'EveryoneInCompanyExcludingGuests' -or
                        $autoAdmit -eq 'EveryoneInSameAndFederatedCompany' -or
                        $autoAdmit -eq 'OrganizerOnly' -or $autoAdmit -eq 'InvitedUsers'
        $isRestricted | Should -Be $true `
            -Because "ISO A.8.4 requires access controls on meetings - lobby bypass should not allow 'Everyone' to bypass"

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Lobby bypass must be restricted to org members only (not everyone)
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
The meeting lobby provides a gate where organizers control when external attendees can join. Allowing anyone to bypass the lobby gives external participants immediate access to the meeting without organizer approval.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: Teams admin center > Meetings > Meeting policies > Global > Who can bypass the lobby > People in my org

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Manage meeting policies for participants and guests](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoftteams/meeting-policies-participants-and-guests)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Dial-in users must not bypass the meeting lobby
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Dial-in users authenticate only with a phone number and meeting code, with no organizational identity verification. Allowing them to bypass the lobby grants unverified attendees direct meeting access.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: Teams admin center > Meetings > Meeting policies > Global > Dial-in users can bypass the lobby > Off

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Manage meeting policies for participants and guests](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoftteams/meeting-policies-participants-and-guests)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: External participants must not be able to give or request control
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Screen sharing and presentation control handed to external participants can be used to display malicious content, perform social engineering, or take control of the meeting flow. Restricting this capability prevents external parties from controlling the meeting presentation.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: Teams admin center > Meetings > Meeting policies > Global > External participants can give or request control > Off

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Teams meeting recording](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoftteams/meeting-policies-recording-and-transcription)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Default meeting presenter role must be OrganizerOnly
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Permanent eligible assignments allow role activation at any time without expiry. Time-bound eligible assignments limit the window during which a compromised account can activate a privileged role — after expiry, the assignment must be renewed through a governed process.

#### Remediation action:

Set a time-bound expiry on eligible assignments for Tier-0 roles. . Remove existing permanent eligible assignments and reissue as time-bound

Configure via: Entra admin center > Identity Governance > PIM > Microsoft Entra roles > select role > Settings > Edit > Assignment tab > Expire eligible assignments after (e.g., 12 months)

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Configure Microsoft Entra role settings in PIM](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/id-governance/privileged-identity-management/pim-how-to-change-default-settings)

- [Microsoft Learn — Assign Microsoft Entra roles in PIM](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/id-governance/privileged-identity-management/pim-how-to-add-role-to-user)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: All Global Administrators must have MFA registered
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Applications with expired credentials remain registered in the tenant but may still be trusted by services that have not rotated their token cache. Expired credential remnants also indicate that application lifecycle management processes are not operating correctly.

#### Remediation action:

Remove expired credentials. Expired secrets/certs are attack surface -- they indicate poor credential lifecycle management

Configure via: Entra admin center > App registrations > Certificates & secrets

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Register an application and create a service principal](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity-platform/howto-create-service-principal-portal)

- [Microsoft Learn — Review and take action on application permissions in Microsoft Entra ID](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/enterprise-apps/manage-application-permissions)

Tests.Block: ISO 27001 - Entra ID Password & MFA Settings

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Telephony-based authentication methods (SMS and Voice) must be disabled
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Per-user MFA is a legacy enforcement method that applies MFA independently of Conditional Access. It does not support modern features like risk-based enforcement, device trust, or named location exceptions. Running both per-user MFA and CA simultaneously creates conflicts and gaps.

#### Remediation action:

Use Conditional Access policies for MFA enforcement instead of per-user MFA

Configure via: Entra admin center > Users > Per-user MFA > Ensure all users show Disabled

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Enable per-user Microsoft Entra multifactor authentication to secure sign-in events](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/authentication/howto-mfa-userstates)

Tests.Block: ISO 27001 - Entra ID Password & MFA Settings

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Email OTP authentication must be disabled
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Email OTP authentication sends a one-time code to an email address, which may itself not be MFA-protected. If the email account is compromised, the email OTP provides no additional security — it's a second factor that is only as strong as the first.

#### Remediation action:

Email OTP is a weaker authentication factor

Configure via: Entra admin center > Protection > Authentication methods > Email OTP > Disable

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Email one-time passcode authentication](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/authentication/how-to-authentication-email-otp)

- [Microsoft Learn — Manage authentication methods for Microsoft Entra ID](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/authentication/how-to-authentication-methods-manage)

Tests.Block: ISO 27001 - Entra ID Password & MFA Settings

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Custom banned password lists must be enabled
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
LAPS randomizes the local administrator password on each managed device, preventing credential reuse attacks. Without LAPS, all managed devices share the same local admin password — if one device is compromised, that credential provides local admin access to every other device.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: Entra admin center > Devices > Device settings > Enable Microsoft Entra Local Administrator Password Solution (LAPS) > Yes

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Manage device identities using the Microsoft Entra admin center](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/devices/device-management-azure-portal)

Tests.Block: ISO 27001 - Entra ID Password & MFA Settings

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Smart Lockout threshold must be 10 or fewer attempts
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Mailboxes hidden from the Global Address List (GAL) cannot be found through normal directory lookup, which may indicate misconfiguration, legacy service accounts with active mailboxes, or deliberate concealment. All active mailboxes should be visible and accounted for in the directory.

#### Remediation action:

Investigate hidden user mailboxes. Mailboxes hidden from the Global Address List may indicate a compromised account. Review: Get-Mailbox -Filter "HiddenFromAddressListsEnabled -eq $true -and RecipientTypeDetails -eq UserMailbox"

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Hide recipients from address lists in Exchange Online](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/exchange/recipients-in-exchange-online/manage-user-mailboxes/hide-from-address-lists)

Tests.Block: ISO 27001 - Entra ID Password & MFA Settings

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Verified domains must have passwords set to never expire (with MFA)
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Apps with active client credentials (secrets or certificates) have a persistent authentication mechanism that does not require user interaction. A leaked or brute-forced credential provides persistent API access without any user sign-in event or MFA challenge.

#### Remediation action:

Remove secrets/certificates from apps that no longer need them

Configure via: Entra admin center > Enterprise applications > review each app with credentials

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Manage consent to applications and evaluate consent requests](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/enterprise-apps/manage-consent-requests)

- [Microsoft Learn — Configure the admin consent workflow](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/enterprise-apps/configure-admin-consent-workflow)

Tests.Block: ISO 27001 - Entra ID Password & MFA Settings

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Authenticator fatigue protection (number matching + app info context) must be enabled
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
App instance property lock prevents non-owner users from modifying app properties across tenants in multi-tenant apps. Without it, tenant administrators in other tenants can modify the app's configuration.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: Entra admin center > Applications > App management policies > configure a default policy to lock sensitive properties on multi-tenant apps

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Hide an application from user's portal in Microsoft Entra ID](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/enterprise-apps/hide-application-from-user-portal)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Infected file download must be blocked in SharePoint/OneDrive
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
SharePoint file storage can be used to host and distribute malware if infected file quarantine is disabled. A malware-infected document uploaded to SharePoint can be synced to all users who have access to the library, spreading the infection across the organization.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: SharePoint admin center > Policies > Malware protection

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — How SharePoint safeguards your data](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sharepoint/safeguarding-your-data)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Maximum device quota per user must be 15 or fewer
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Per-user MFA is a legacy enforcement method that applies MFA independently of Conditional Access. It does not support modern features like risk-based enforcement, device trust, or named location exceptions. Running both per-user MFA and CA simultaneously creates conflicts and gaps.

#### Remediation action:

Use Conditional Access policies for MFA enforcement instead of per-user MFA

Configure via: Entra admin center > Users > Per-user MFA > Ensure all users show Disabled

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Enable per-user Microsoft Entra multifactor authentication to secure sign-in events](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/authentication/howto-mfa-userstates)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: At least one device compliance policy must be configured
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
A CA policy needs at least one valid subject to evaluate against. If all included users or groups have been deleted, the policy applies to an empty set and is effectively disabled. This means controls like MFA, compliant device, or session frequency that the policy was meant to enforce are silently absent.

#### Remediation action:

For each enabled policy, verify the Users > Include section contains at least one valid, resolvable user, group, or role. Policies with empty include targets should be disabled or remediated with a valid fallback include (e.g., 'All users')

Configure via: Entra admin center > Protection > Conditional Access > Policies

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Users and groups in Conditional Access policy](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/conditional-access/concept-conditional-access-users-groups)

- [Maester — MT.1036 CA policy has at least one valid include user](https://maester.dev/docs/tests/EIDSCA)

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Local Administrator Password Solution (LAPS) must be enabled in Entra ID
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
LAPS (Local Administrator Password Solution) randomizes and manages local admin passwords on Windows devices. Without restricting who can retrieve LAPS passwords, over-privileged users can access local admin credentials for any device and use them for lateral movement.

#### Remediation action:

Minimize additional local admins

Configure via: Entra admin center > Devices > Device settings > Manage Additional local administrators on all Azure AD joined devices

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Manage device identities using the Microsoft Entra admin center](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/devices/device-management-azure-portal)

Tests.Block: ISO 27001 - Entra ID App Consent & Group Settings

ResultDetail.TestTitle: User consent for applications must be blocked
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Safe Links rewrites URLs in emails and Office documents and checks them at click time against Microsoft's threat intelligence. Without it, URLs pointing to malicious sites — including late-stage weaponized links that appear safe at delivery — are not blocked when the user clicks.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: Security admin center > Safe Links > Create a policy covering all users

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Safe Links in Microsoft Defender for Office 365](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/defender-office-365/safe-links-about)

- [Microsoft Learn — Set up Safe Links policies](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/defender-office-365/safe-links-policies-configure)

Tests.Block: ISO 27001 - Entra ID App Consent & Group Settings

ResultDetail.TestTitle: Users must not be allowed to register custom applications
ResultDetail.TestDescription:
Allowing any user to register applications enables low-privilege users to create service principals with arbitrary permissions. Malicious insiders or compromised users can register apps as a persistence mechanism or to request elevated permissions via admin consent.

#### Remediation action:

Configure via: Entra admin center > Users > User settings

#### Related links

- [Microsoft Learn — Restrict who can create app registrations](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/enterprise-apps/restrict-app-registration)

- [Microsoft Learn — Register an application with the Microsoft identity platform](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity-platform/quickstart-register-app)
